steps.title: Express the four consecutive odd integers
steps.content:
Since the first is $x$, and consecutive odd integers differ by 2:
  • 1st: $x$
  • 2nd: $x+2$
  • 3rd: $x+4$
  • 4th: $x+6$

steps.title: Translate the constraint to an inequality
steps.content:
Breaking down: The product of 12 and the fourth is at most 26 less than the sum of the first and third.

  • Product of 12 and the fourth: $12(x+6)$
  • Sum of first and third: $x+(x+4)$
  • 26 less than that sum: $x+(x+4)-26$
  • Is at most means: $\le$

Write the inequality:
$$12(x+6) \le x+(x+4)-26$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
